records.ety: [AS.; akin to D., G., OS., Sw., & Dan. blind, Icel. blindr, Goth. blinds; of uncertain origin.]

defs: Destitute of the sense of seeing, either by natural defect or by deprivation; without sight.
quotes: He that is strucken blind can not forget The precious treasure of his eyesight lost.

defs: Not having the faculty of discernment; destitute of intellectual light; unable or unwilling to understand or judge; as, authors are blind to their own defects.
quotes: But hard be hardened, blind be blinded more, That they may stumble on, and deeper fall.
This plan is recommended neither to blind approbation nor to blind reprobation.

defs: Having such a state or condition as a thing would have to a person who is blind; not well marked or easily discernible; hidden; unseen; concealed; as, a blind path; a blind ditch.
Involved; intricate; not easily followed or traced.
quotes: The blind mazes of this tangled wood.

defs: Having no openings for light or passage; as, a blind wall; open only at one end; as, a blind alley; a blind gut.
Unintelligible, or not easily intelligible; as, a blind passage in a book; illegible; as, blind writing.
Abortive; failing to produce flowers or fruit; as, blind buds; blind flowers.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Blinded; p. pr. & vb. n. Blinding.]

defs: To make blind; to deprive of sight or discernment.
quotes: A blind guide is certainly a great mischief; but a guide that blinds those whom he should lead is . . . a much greater.

defs: To deprive partially of vision; to make vision difficult for and painful to; to dazzle.
quotes: Her beauty all the rest did blind.

defs: To darken; to obscure to the eye or understanding; to conceal; to deceive.
quotes: The state of the controversy between us he endeavored, with all his art, to blind and confound.

defs: To cover with a thin coating of sand and fine gravel; as a road newly paved, in order that the joints between the stones may be filled.

records.ety: [Cf. F. blindes, prob. fr. G. blende, fr. blenden to blind, fr. blind blind.]

defs: Something to hinder sight or keep out light; a screen; a cover; esp. a hinged screen or shutter for a window; a blinder for a horse.
Something to mislead the eye or the understanding, or to conceal some covert deed or design; a subterfuge.
